name: GaitSpan: Growing Humanoid Locomotion from Walking to Running
summary: arXiv:2607.12114v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: A humanoid that can walk should not relearn locomotion from scratch to jog or run. Yet current approaches often obtain gait diversity by prescribin...
description: arXiv:2607.12114v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: A humanoid that can walk should not relearn locomotion from scratch to jog or run. Yet current approaches often obtain gait diversity by prescribing gait schedules, imitating motion clips, training experts to switch between or distilling skills into one policy. These strategies can produce impressive behaviors, but offer limited flexibility acro...
author: Kwan-Yee Lin, Zilin Wang, Janelle J. Liu, Stella X. Yu
